reason: All FR-24 acceptance criteria are satisfied. GOLDEN_DATASET contains 510 entries (6 × 85, range(1,86)), exceeding the 500-record minimum. Each category has 85 entries, well above the 50-per-category floor. Import-time assertions enforce both invariants at load. Each dict entry carries the required query/expected_intent/expected_answer fields. EdgeCaseCollector.ingest() stores those three fields; approve() sets status='approved' and annotated_at to a UTC ISO timestamp; mark_for_regression() sets used_in_regression=TRUE; get_regression_set() filters WHERE used_in_regression=TRUE AND status='approved', matching the AC exactly. EdgeCase dataclass captures all six required columns. get_category() correctly classifies all six boundary types via substring matching that aligns with the generated query patterns. MockPool/MockConnection in the test file provide full coverage of all four DB methods without requiring a live database. Minor observation: no bulk-seed utility is shipped to auto-approve and flag all 510 entries from GOLDEN_DATASET in one call; callers must loop and invoke approve()+mark_for_regression() per record. This is a usability gap, not an AC gap, since the AC specifies the schema state rather than a seeding mechanism, and the tools to reach that state are all present.
gaps: No bulk-seed helper to ingest GOLDEN_DATASET into DB with status=approved and used_in_regression=TRUE in one call; callers must implement the loop manually.
